title: Ebenus micrantha
intro: Ebenus micrantha, also known as the Small-flowered Ebenus, is a flowering plant species belonging to the Fabaceae family, characterized by its small, pink flowers and deciduous leaves. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Ebenus micrantha include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Ebenus micrantha is commonly used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including fever, coughs, and colds.
taxonomy: Ebenus micrantha is classified within the family Fabaceae. This flowering plant belongs to the genus Ebenus. The species Ebenus micrantha is native to the Mediterranean region, which may influence its classification as a regional endemic. Further classification may include its division within the subfamily Faboideae. As part of the Fabaceae family, Ebenus micrantha shares characteristics with other legume species.
uses: Ebenus micrantha is native to South Africa and has been traditionally used in folk medicine.
